User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the SheetToTasks AI application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user entity.

googleId.description: The Google ID of the user from Firebase Authentication.

Spreadsheet.title: Spreadsheet
Spreadsheet.description: Represents a spreadsheet uploaded by the user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the spreadsheet entity.

name.description: Name of the uploaded spreadsheet file.

uploadDate.description: Date and time when the spreadsheet was uploaded.

MappingTemplate.title: MappingTemplate
MappingTemplate.description: Represents a mapping template created by the user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the mapping template entity.

templateData.description: JSON string containing the mapping configuration (column mappings, date formats, etc.).

description.description: A user-friendly description of what the template does.

TaskSync.title: TaskSync
TaskSync.description: Represents a synchronization event between a spreadsheet and a task management platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the task sync event.

syncDate.description: Date and time when the synchronization occurred.

destinationPlatform.description: The task management platform to which the tasks were synced (e.g., 'Google Tasks', 'Notion', 'Todoist').

successCount.description: The number of tasks successfully exported.

failureCount.description: The number of tasks that failed to export.

GoogleTasksIntegration.title: GoogleTasksIntegration
GoogleTasksIntegration.description: Represents a Google Tasks integration for a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Google Tasks integration entity.

accessToken.description: OAuth 2.0 access token for Google Tasks API.

refreshToken.description: OAuth 2.0 refresh token for Google Tasks API.

expirationDate.description: Date and time when the access token expires.

taskListId.description: ID of the Google Tasks list to sync to by default.

NotionIntegration.title: NotionIntegration
NotionIntegration.description: Represents a Notion integration for a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Notion integration entity.

databaseId.description: ID of the Notion database to sync to.

TodoistIntegration.title: TodoistIntegration
TodoistIntegration.description: Represents a Todoist integration for a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Todoist integration entity.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. Path-based ownership ensures only the user can access their own profile.

params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: mappingTemplateId

params.name: taskSyncId
params.description: The unique identifier of the task sync event.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore data structure is designed to ensure security, scalability, and ease of debugging, adhering to the core design principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC (Database-Based Access Control), and QAPs (Rules are not Filters). Authorization Independence is achieved through denormalization. For instance, any data requiring authorization based on user membership or ownership denormalizes the relevant user IDs or roles directly within the document or subcollection. Structural Segregation is used to group data with similar access patterns into dedicated collections. Access Modeling utilizes path-based ownership for user-specific data and membership maps for collaborative data. Explicit state modeling uses a `status` field where appropriate. This design avoids hierarchical `get()` calls in security rules, enabling atomic operations and improving debuggability. QAPs are addressed by segregating data based on access requirements, enabling efficient and secure list operations. The structure uses hierarchical paths to represent User 1:N relationships, ensuring clear ownership and simplified security rules.
